title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt
summary: A guide to becoming a better software developer through pragmatic techniques and habits.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
summary: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship focused on writing readable, maintainable code.

title: Design Patterns
author: Erich Gamma
summary: The classic catalogue of reusable object-oriented design patterns by the Gang of Four.

title: The Go Programming Language
author: Alan Donovan
summary: The authoritative resource on the Go language, written by members of the Go team.

title: Lucene in Action
author: Erik Hatcher
summary: An in-depth guide to building search applications with Apache Lucene.

title: Introduction to Information Retrieval
author: Christopher Manning
summary: A textbook covering the foundations of information retrieval, indexing and ranking.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
summary: Big ideas behind reliable, scalable and maintainable systems for storing and processing data.

title: Refactoring
author: Martin Fowler
summary: A catalogue of techniques for improving the design of existing code without changing behaviour.

title: The Mythical Man-Month
author: Frederick Brooks
summary: Essays on software engineering and the perils of large projects, including Brooks' law.

title: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
author: Harold Abelson
summary: A foundational text on the principles of computation taught using Scheme.

title: Compilers: Principles, Techniques, and Tools
author: Alfred V. Aho
summary: The Dragon Book: a comprehensive reference on compiler construction.

title: Programming Pearls
author: Jon Bentley
summary: Short essays on practical programming problems and elegant algorithmic solutions.
